_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-13 Electronic Records and EMS Information System (Revised 5/30/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: Electronic Records and EMS Information System
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-13: Electronic Records and EMS Information System — MDHHS Approved 5/30/2023
purpose: Govern EMS electronic patient care record (ePCR) and EMS information system (EMSIS) requirements for Michigan EMS providers. All Michigan licensed EMS agencies are required to submit ePCR data to MDHHS through the Michigan EMSIS. Data submission supports quality improvement, public health surveillance, and system performance evaluation.

sections.title: ePCR Requirements
points: All EMS agencies licensed in Michigan are required to maintain and submit ePCR data
ePCR must be completed for every EMS response — including responses that do not result in transport
ePCR data elements must conform to NEMSIS (National EMS Information System) standards as adopted by Michigan EMSIS
ePCRs must be completed within the timeframe specified by the MCA (see Documentation Protocol, Section 7-16)
System downtime: paper PCR must be available as a backup; paper records must be transcribed into ePCR when system is restored

sections.title: EMSIS Data Submission
points: Michigan EMSIS: all Michigan licensed EMS agencies must submit ePCR data to the Michigan EMSIS
Data submission must be compliant with Michigan EMSIS data submission standards
Agencies must submit data in a timely manner — delays in submission may affect MCA QI processes and MDHHS reporting
MDHHS uses EMSIS data for: state EMS system oversight, quality improvement, and public health surveillance

sections.title: Data Security and Patient Privacy
points: ePCR data is protected health information (PHI) — must be handled per HIPAA and Michigan privacy laws
Access to ePCR systems must be restricted to authorized personnel
ePCR systems must have audit logs tracking who accessed patient records and when
Lost or stolen devices containing ePCR data must be reported per agency breach notification policies
Refer to Section 8-14: Protected Health Information Protocol for additional PHI requirements

scope_notes: ePCR required for every EMS response — including no-transport calls
Paper backup required for system downtime; transcribe to ePCR when restored
All Michigan agencies must submit to Michigan EMSIS
ePCR is PHI — HIPAA and Michigan privacy law protections apply
Refer to Section 8-14 for additional PHI requirements
